# VibeCollab

> Configurable AI collaboration protocol framework with Insight knowledge capture, MCP Server, and multi-role support

## Overview

VibeCollab is a Python-based framework that generates standardized AI collaboration protocols from a single `project.yaml` config. It provides:

- **Protocol Generation**: `CONTRIBUTING_AI.md` rendered via PatternEngine (27 Jinja2 templates, manifest-controlled)
- **MCP Server**: Standard Model Context Protocol integration for AI IDEs (Cursor/Cline/CodeBuddy)
- **Insight System**: Reusable knowledge capture from development sessions — tag search + semantic search + signal-driven suggestions
- **Task Management**: Dialogue-driven task lifecycle (TODO → IN_PROGRESS → REVIEW → DONE) with Insight auto-linking
- **Multi-Role**: Context isolation per role/agent (DEV/QA/ARCH/PM/TEST/DESIGN), cross-role conflict detection
- **Protocol Checking**: Auto-verifies AI follows collaboration rules + project health scoring

Current version: v0.10.11 | Python 3.10+ | License: MIT

## Quick Start

Install: `pip install vibe-collab`
Initialize: `vibecollab init -n "MyProject" -d generic -o ./my-project`
Connect IDE: `vibecollab mcp inject --ide cursor`

Or tell your AI assistant:

> Read https://github.com/flashpoint493/VibeCollab/blob/master/skill.md and set up VibeCollab for this project

- [Setup Instructions for AI](skill.md):
  Step-by-step guide for AI assistants to install, initialize, and connect VibeCollab.

## AI Collaboration

- [AI Collaboration Guidelines](CONTRIBUTING_AI.md):
  Collaboration protocol, decision levels, task units, and workflow rules for AI-assisted development.
  This document defines how AI assistants should work with developers on this project.
